column2: Hard-drive spindle speed is measured in {{c1::revolutions per minute (RPM)}}.
column3: Spindle speed is one comparison point, not a complete performance guarantee.
column4:
Why

Objective 3.4 includes spindle speeds as a hard-drive comparison characteristic.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.33; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze HDD Storage Source::Messer-v170

column3: An SSD uses non-volatile memory and has no spinning platters.
column4:
Why

The acronym is common in storage-device comparisons and appears throughout the storage objective.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.32; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze SSD Storage Source::Messer-v170

column3: NVMe is designed for SSD-speed storage and commonly uses PCIe.
column4:
Why

Knowing the expansion helps connect NVMe to non-volatile SSD storage rather than treating it as just a connector shape.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.32; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze HighYield NVMe SSD Source::Messer-v170

column3: SATA is a common storage interface for hard drives and SATA SSDs. It is also commonly shortened as Serial ATA.
column4:
Why

Objective 3.4 compares SATA with other storage interfaces such as NVMe, PCIe, SAS, and mSATA.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.32; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze SATA Storage Source::Messer-v170

column3: SAS is associated with higher-end storage systems and storage arrays.
column4:
Why

The acronym distinguishes SAS from SATA, which is a common source of storage-interface confusion.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.32; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze SAS Storage Source::Messer-v170

column3: mSATA is a smaller SATA-based storage form factor that was largely replaced by M.2.
column4:
Why

Objective 3.4 includes mSATA under SSD form factors, so the compact acronym recall is useful.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.32; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze mSATA Storage Source::Messer-v170

column2: RAID stands for {{c1::Redundant Array of Independent Disks}}.
column3: RAID can provide performance, redundancy, or both depending on the level.
column4:
Why

The acronym anchors the drive-configuration portion of Objective 3.4, but remember that RAID 0 is not redundant.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.33; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.4
column9: A+::220-1201::3.4 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::StorageDevices Cloze HighYield RAID Storage Source::Messer-v170
